refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: supporting achievement of a specified cycle time target for a defined operational cycle. It is expressed in the problem domain (what the system must do) without specifying how to achieve it, thus maintaining solution neutrality. The FR is atomic as it addresses one measurable performance requirement related to cycle time.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load→travel→dump cycle under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, and verify that the 95th percentile (p95) cycle time is less than or equal to the customer-defined target (CT_target).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load handling mechanism to minimize load time
Optimize travel mechanism to minimize travel time
Optimize dump mechanism to minimize dump time
Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time
Implement cycle time monitoring and adaptive control to meet target
selected_dp.selected_dp: Coordinate load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time

potential_sources: Interdependence between load, travel, and dump phases timing
coupling_check.notes: Selecting coordination as DP explicitly addresses the integration of sub-operations to meet overall cycle time, reducing risk of sub-optimization and coupling between phases.

assumptions_risks: Cycle time can be decomposed into load, travel, and dump phases with measurable durations
Operational policies and safety plans do not impose constraints that prevent coordination optimization
Measurement of cycle time p95 is reliable under duty cycle and site conditions
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining the FR to explicitly include coordination or sequencing of sub-operations to clarify the DP focus on integrated cycle time optimization.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Coordinates load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time across the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges operational data for coordination and monitoring

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and task assignments for operation coordination

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Monitors and communicates safety-related alerts during operations

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between distributed modules for coordination

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as it encompasses overall operation coordination; minimal diffusion as it centralizes coordination logic.
Partial containment since vehicle executes operations but coordination logic would diffuse to other modules.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on remote control, not overall coordination.
Minimal containment; safety services monitor alerts but do not coordinate operations.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it is designed to coordinate complex operational tasks across vehicles and systems, minimizing cycle time effectively within a single cohesive module.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may lead to integration challenges.
Coordination logic might require frequent updates impacting module stability.
Dependency on real-time data from multiple interfaces could increase coupling risk.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks are high, consider refining the DP to separate coordination of load/travel/dump into sub-functions that can be modularized within NGHE Solution to improve maintainability without losing cohesion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on coordinating load, travel, and dump operations to minimize total cycle time, directly supporting the customer-defined cycle time target. This mapping is clear and grounded in the acceptance criteria of achieving p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target under specified conditions.

diffusion_risks: Coordination logic might diffuse if sub-functions are not well modularized within the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion risk (score 2), indicating effective centralization of the DP. Other modules have lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through measuring cycle time performance (p95 ≤ CT_target) and monitoring operational data via the provided interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear, as it encompasses coordination across operations and interfaces, aligning with the FR's requirement for cycle time support and operational policies.

recommended_revisions: Consider decomposing the DP into sub-functions for load, travel, and dump coordination within the NGHE Solution module to reduce complexity and improve maintainability without sacrificing cohesion.
Monitor and manage dependencies on real-time data interfaces to minimize coupling and integration risks.
Regularly review and update coordination logic to maintain module stability and performance.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current FR, DP, and IM mappings are sound and verification is feasible. However, the recommended revisions suggest decomposing the DP into sub-functions for load, travel, and dump coordination to reduce complexity and improve maintainability. Given the high safety criticality, further decomposition will help isolate functions, reduce diffusion risk, and enhance module stability. Therefore, it is appropriate to derive next-level sub-FRs corresponding to these coordination sub-functions to enable detailed design and risk management.
next_level_sub_frs: Coordinate Load Operation to Minimize Cycle Time
Coordinate Travel Operation to Minimize Cycle Time
Coordinate Dump Operation to Minimize Cycle Time

revise.why: To decompose the DP into sub-functions for load, travel, and dump coordination within the NGHE Solution module, reducing complexity and improving maintainability while maintaining cohesion.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module is the primary IM boundary for the DP.
Verification of cycle time performance is feasible and reliable.
Coordination logic can be modularized without losing overall system cohesion.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of coordination logic if sub-functions are not well modularized.
Dependencies on real-time data interfaces may increase coupling and integration risks.
evidence_needed: Performance data showing p95 cycle time ≤ CT_target after decomposition.
Verification results of sub-function coordination logic modules.
Assessment of module containment and diffusion scores post-decomposition.
